---
title: "Presentation Example"
author: Victor van Pelt
email: victor.vanpelt@whu.edu
institute: "WHU – Otto Beisheim School of Management"
date: today
date-format: long
pdf-engine: xelatex   # xelatex is needed for the custom fonts; pdflatex rasters them
format:
  beamer:
    slide_level: 3
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
bibliography: "references.bib"
header-includes:
# This uses a beamer theme edited for WHU. Be my guest to use and change it,
# or swap in a standard theme (e.g. format: beamer: theme: Madrid).
- \input{materials/beamer_theme_16x9.sty}
- \usepackage{booktabs}
- \usepackage{wrapfig}
- \usepackage{graphicx}
- \usepackage{subcaption}
---

## Import table

```{=tex}

\input{../3_output/table_1.tex}

```

## Summary

-   We study whether generative AI use is associated with (scaled) earnings.
-   We regress scaled earnings on a generative AI indicator with heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.
-   We find a positive and statistically significant association (see Table 1).
-   The estimate, the table, and the paper all update automatically when the underlying data change.

## Thank you!

```{=tex}
\begin{wrapfigure}{r}{0.37\textwidth}
  \vspace{-0.5cm}
  \includegraphics[width=0.41\textwidth]{"materials/profile.jpg"}
\end{wrapfigure}
```
Dr. Victor van Pelt\
Assistant Professor of Accounting\
Finance and Accounting Group\
WHU – Otto Beisheim School of Management

Campus Vallendar, Burgplatz 2, 56179 Vallendar, Germany\
Tel.: +49 (0)261 6509 483\
[Victor.vanPelt\@whu.edu](mailto:Victor.vanPelt@whu.edu)\
<https://www.victorvanpelt.com>
